title: The Detailing of Mr. Steele
introKo:
동네 디테일링 샵. 정비사 Steele 씨가 보닛 안과 광택을 유난히 꼼꼼하게 봐주는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.
introEn:
A very thorough car detailing with Mr. Steele. Strictly about the car.
...on the surface.

quiz.q: Steele 씨가 손세차에서 가장 중요하다고 한 곳은?

normal.text: Saturday morning. You pull into Steele's Auto Detailing for the first time. Mr. Steele wipes his hands on a rag and looks your car over slowly.
"Nice ride," he says, walking a slow circle around it. "For the next hour, she is all mine. Let's make her shine."
"First, let's pop the hood," he says. "I like to see what I am working with underneath. Don't be shy — open her up."

options.en: I've been waiting all week for this. Open it up.
options.ko: 이 순간을 일주일 내내 기다렸어요. 열어 주세요.
options.reaction: "That is what I like to hear," he grins. "Eager. This will be fun."

options.en: Go easy on her — it's my first time getting detailed.
options.reaction: "First time?" He smiles. "Don't worry. I will walk you through every step."

normal.text: Under the hood, he checks the oil with careful fingers. "A little low," he murmurs. "Let me take good care of that for you."
"Now for the wax," he says, holding up a tin. "A good coat, rubbed in deep, and she will shine for weeks. The more I give her, the better she looks."

options.en: Just a light coat is fine, thank you.
options.reaction: "Light and gentle," he nods. "Some people like it slow. I can do slow."

options.en: Don't hold back. Give her everything you have.
options.ko: 아끼지 말고 가진 거 다 쏟아부어 주세요.
options.reaction: "Everything?" He laughs. "Now that is my kind of customer."

normal.text: Next comes the hand wash. He works the sponge into every curve and every narrow gap. "The corners matter most," he says. "That is where the real dirt hides."
"I have to reach these tight spots before the water dries," he says, leaning close. "Everything goes better while she is still warm."

options.en: Don't stop until every spot is done.
options.ko: 구석구석 다 끝날 때까지 멈추지 마세요.
options.reaction: "I never leave a spot untouched," he says. "That is a promise."

options.en: Take your time. There is no rush at all.
options.reaction: "Slow and thorough," he says. "That is the only way I know."

normal.text: Finally, the polish. He buffs the hood in slow circles until it glows. "Almost there," he breathes. "The best shine always comes at the very end."
He steps back. The car gleams like new, catching every ray of light. "Now that," he says softly, "is a job done right."

options.en: She has never looked better. Thank you, Mr. Steele.
options.ko: 이렇게 반짝인 적이 없어요. 고마워요, Steele 씨.
options.reaction: "My pleasure," he says. "A good shine is its own reward."

options.en: I want this every week. Can I book you for good?
options.ko: 매주 이거 받고 싶어요. 단골로 예약해도 돼요?
options.reaction: "For you?" He pockets your key with a wink. "My door is always open."

normal.text: You drive home in a car that purrs and gleams. That night you sleep easy, already counting the days until next Saturday.

hard.text: Saturday morning finds you pulling into Steele's Auto Detailing for the very first time. Mr. Steele wipes his hands on a rag and studies your car the way an artist studies a blank canvas.
"Now that is a fine machine," he says, circling it slowly. "For the next hour, she belongs entirely to me. Let's bring out a shine you have never seen before."
"We begin by popping the hood," he says. "I always like to see exactly what I am working with underneath. Don't be shy now — open her right up."

options.en: I've been waiting all week for this. Open it up.
options.ko: 이 순간을 일주일 내내 기다렸어요. 열어 주세요.
options.reaction: "That is exactly what I like to hear," he grins. "Eager customers are always the most fun."

options.en: Go easy on her — it's my first time getting detailed.
options.reaction: "Your first time?" He smiles warmly. "Then relax. I will walk you through every single step."

hard.text: Under the hood, he checks the oil with slow, careful fingers. "Running a touch low," he murmurs. "Let me take very good care of that for you."
"Now for the wax," he says, lifting a tin. "A generous coat, rubbed in nice and deep, and she will shine for weeks. The more I give her, the better she behaves."

options.en: Just a light coat will be fine, thank you.
options.reaction: "Light and slow," he nods. "Some people prefer to take their time. I have no problem with slow."

options.en: Don't hold back. Give her absolutely everything.
options.ko: 아끼지 말고 가진 거 다 쏟아부어 주세요.
options.reaction: "Everything?" He laughs under his breath. "Now you are speaking my language."

hard.text: Next comes the hand wash. He works the sponge into every curve and every narrow gap without missing a thing. "The corners always matter most," he says. "That is where the real dirt likes to hide."
"I need to reach these tight little spots before the water dries," he says, leaning in close. "Trust me — everything goes far better while she is still warm."

options.en: Don't stop until every last spot is done.
options.ko: 구석구석 하나도 빠짐없이 끝날 때까지 멈추지 마세요.
options.reaction: "I have never once left a spot untouched," he says. "You have my word on that."

options.en: Take all the time you need. There is no rush at all.
options.ko: 필요한 만큼 천천히 하세요. 하나도 안 급해요.
options.reaction: "Slow and thorough," he says. "Honestly, it is the only way I know how to work."

hard.text: At last, the polish. He buffs the hood in slow, patient circles until the metal begins to glow. "Almost there," he breathes. "The very best shine always saves itself for the end."
He steps back to admire his work. The car gleams like something brand new, catching every ray of the morning light. "Now that," he says softly, "is what I call a job done properly."

options.en: She has never looked better. Thank you, Mr. Steele.
options.ko: 이렇게 반짝인 적이 없어요. 고마워요, Steele 씨.
options.reaction: "The pleasure is all mine," he says. "A shine like this is its own reward."

options.en: I want this every single week. Can I book you for good?
options.ko: 매주 이거 받고 싶어요. 단골로 예약해도 돼요?
options.reaction: "For a customer like you?" He pockets your key with a wink. "My door is always open."

hard.text: You drive home in a car that purrs beneath you and gleams in every mirror. That night you sleep easily, already counting the days until next Saturday comes around.

0000.name: 조심스러운 첫 손님
0000.en: You kept it gentle from start to finish and drove off with a polite thank-you. Every few months your car quietly reappears at Steele's, always for the light package. Some shines, you have decided, are best enjoyed slowly and rarely.
0000.ko: 처음부터 끝까지 조심스럽게 받고, 정중한 인사만 남기고 떠났어요. 몇 달에 한 번씩 당신 차가 조용히 Steele's 앞에 다시 나타나요. 늘 가벼운 코스로요. 어떤 광택은 천천히, 가끔 즐기는 게 제일이라고 당신은 정했죠.

0001.name: 다시 오는 조용한 단골
0001.en: You were shy with the hood, light on the wax, and easy on the corners — yet you still booked next Saturday before you left. "Careful hands," Mr. Steele tells the other detailers, "but he always comes back."
0001.ko: 보닛 앞에선 수줍고, 왁스는 가볍게, 구석은 살살. 그래도 떠나기 전에 다음 토요일을 예약했죠. "손은 조심스러워도 꼭 다시 온다니까." Steele 씨가 다른 정비사들에게 말해요.

0010.name: 말없이 꼼꼼한 사람
0010.en: You said little all morning, but you told him one thing clearly: do not stop until every spot is done. Mr. Steele remembers that. The hardest, dirtiest corners now wait especially for you.
0010.ko: 아침 내내 말수는 적었지만, 딱 하나는 분명히 말했죠. 구석구석 다 끝날 때까지 멈추지 말라고요. Steele 씨는 그걸 기억해요. 이제 제일 지저분하고 까다로운 구석은 늘 당신을 기다려요.

0011.name: 은근한 완벽주의자
0011.en: You began shy and modest, but by the end you wanted every corner perfect and booked to do it all again. Mr. Steele loves that kind of customer. "Quiet at the gate, particular by the polish," he says. "My favorite type."
0011.ko: 수줍고 소박하게 시작했지만, 끝엔 구석 하나까지 완벽하길 바랐고 다시 오겠다고 예약까지 했죠. Steele 씨는 그런 손님을 좋아해요. "들어올 땐 조용하다가 광낼 땐 까다로운 것 — 제일 좋아하는 유형이에요." 그가 말해요.

0100.name: 왁스 인심 좋은 손님
0100.en: You were careful with everything except the wax — "give her everything," you said, and he did. Mr. Steele still laughs about it. His shelf has never run so low on wax, and your car has never gleamed so bright.
0100.ko: 다른 건 다 조심스러웠는데 왁스만은 아니었어요. "가진 거 다 부어 주세요"라던 그 한마디에 그는 정말 그렇게 했죠. Steele 씨는 아직도 그 얘길 하며 웃어요. 왁스 선반이 이렇게 바닥난 적이 없고, 당신 차가 이렇게 눈부신 적도 없었대요.

0101.name: 광을 사랑하는 단골
0101.nameEn: The Regular Who Loves a Shine
0101.en: Every Saturday your car returns, and every Saturday you ask for the deepest coat he has. Gentle in every other way, but never with the wax — never. "When he visits," Mr. Steele says, "my whole shop smells of wax for a week."
0101.ko: 토요일마다 당신 차가 돌아오고, 토요일마다 당신은 그가 가진 제일 깊은 코스를 부탁해요. 다른 건 다 부드럽게, 왁스만은 절대 아니죠. "저 손님이 오는 날엔 가게 전체가 일주일 내내 왁스 냄새예요." Steele 씨가 말해요.

0110.name: 숨은 실력자
0110.en: You arrived shy, but the work drew it out of you: a full coat, every corner spotless, all in one quiet morning. Then you left without booking again, and that is the part Mr. Steele cannot understand. "All that shine," he sighs at the door, "and he only came once."
0110.ko: 수줍게 왔지만, 일이 당신 안의 무언가를 끌어냈죠. 왁스 풀코스에, 구석 하나 없이 완벽하게, 그 조용한 아침 하나에요. 그러고는 다시 예약도 없이 떠났고, Steele 씨는 그게 이해가 안 돼요. "그렇게 광을 내 놓고 딱 한 번 오다니." 그가 문가에서 한숨을 쉬어요.

0111.name: 늦게 불붙는 사람
0111.nameEn: The Slow Starter Who Caught Fire
0111.en: You called it your first time, and maybe it was. But by noon you wanted the full coat, every corner done right, and next Saturday already booked. "Customers like you," Mr. Steele says, "are the reason I still love this work."
0111.ko: 처음이라고 했죠. 정말 그랬을지도 몰라요. 그런데 정오쯤엔 풀코스 왁스에, 구석까지 완벽하게, 다음 토요일까지 이미 예약했어요. "당신 같은 손님 때문에 아직도 이 일이 좋다니까요." Steele 씨가 말해요.

1000.name: 말 앞선 새 손님
1000.en: "I've waited all week for this," you announced — then asked him to go easy, keep it light, and waved a polite goodbye. Mr. Steele found it charming. "Big entrance, gentle heart," he says. "Honestly, my easiest customers talk the loudest."
1000.ko: "이 순간을 일주일 내내 기다렸어요"라더니, 그 뒤로는 살살, 가볍게, 그리고 정중한 작별 인사였죠. Steele 씨는 그게 오히려 귀엽대요. "등장은 요란한데 마음은 순한 것 — 사실 제일 편한 손님이 제일 말이 많아요." 그가 말해요.

1001.name: 약속을 지키는 손님
1001.en: You came in bold, kept things gentle, and did the one thing that counts: you came back. Now every Saturday runs the same — your key, his hands, one perfect shine. "Some men just talk," Mr. Steele tells the shop. "This one shows up."
1001.ko: 당당하게 들어와서, 일은 부드럽게, 그리고 제일 중요한 걸 했죠. 다시 온 거예요. 이제 토요일마다 똑같아요. 당신의 열쇠, 그의 손, 완벽한 광 하나. "말만 하는 남자도 많은데, 이 사람은 진짜로 와요." Steele 씨가 가게에 말해요.

1010.name: 손 빠른 해결사
1010.en: Confident from hello and set on getting every corner done — that is how Mr. Steele describes you now. You never booked again, yet somehow the trickiest jobs always seem to land on the day you happen to drive past. Funny how that works.
1010.ko: 첫인사부터 자신만만, 게다가 구석 하나까지 다 끝내려는 사람. 요즘 Steele 씨가 당신을 그렇게 소개해요. 다시 예약은 안 했는데, 이상하게 제일 까다로운 작업들은 꼭 당신이 지나가는 날에 생겨요. 참 신기한 일이죠.

1011.name: 떠오르는 단골 에이스
1011.en: Bold at the door, thorough to the last corner, and booked for next week before he could offer — the other customers keep asking who gets the special treatment. Mr. Steele just smiles and says nothing at all.
1011.ko: 문 앞에서부터 당당하게, 마지막 구석까지 꼼꼼하게, 그리고 그가 권하기도 전에 다음 주를 예약했죠. 다른 손님들은 누가 그런 특별 대접을 받느냐고 자꾸 물어요. Steele 씨는 그저 웃으며 아무 말도 안 해요.

1100.name: 넘치도록 부어 준 손님
1100.en: You strode in bold and asked for everything he had, and he poured it on like a summer storm — yet you took it easy on the corners and left early with a wave. Mr. Steele still tells the story: "He came, he shined, he drove off glowing." Then he looks at the empty wax tin and grins.
1100.ko: 당당하게 들어와서 가진 걸 다 달라고 했고, 그는 여름 소나기처럼 쏟아부었죠. 그래도 구석은 살살, 손만 흔들고 일찍 떠났어요. Steele 씨는 아직도 그 얘길 해요. "왔고, 광을 냈고, 반짝이며 떠났죠." 그러고는 텅 빈 왁스 통을 보며 씩 웃어요.

1101.name: 광에 진심인 단골
1101.en: Bold from the first hello, generous with every coat, and back every Saturday without fail. The only thing you ever went easy on was the corners — you let him handle those his own slow way, and that is the part he likes best. "The finest shine," he says, "is never rushed."
1101.ko: 첫인사부터 당당하게, 코스마다 아낌없이, 그리고 토요일마다 어김없이 돌아와요. 유일하게 살살 넘어간 건 구석뿐이었죠. 그건 그가 자기만의 느린 방식으로 하게 뒀고, 그가 제일 좋아하는 부분이 바로 그거예요. "제일 좋은 광은 절대 서두르지 않는 법이죠." 그가 말해요.

1110.name: 좋은 꿈 같은 손님
1110.en: Bold, generous, and thorough to the last corner — you did it all, called it the finest shine you had ever seen, and drove away. Mr. Steele stood in the doorway a long while. Some customers, he thinks, are like a good dream: wonderful, and gone far too soon.
1110.ko: 당당하게, 아낌없이, 마지막 구석까지 꼼꼼하게. 전부 해내고는 이렇게 좋은 광은 처음이라며 떠났죠. Steele 씨는 한참 문가에 서 있었어요. 어떤 손님은 좋은 꿈 같아요. 근사한데, 너무 빨리 사라지죠.

1111.name: 샵의 전설이 된 손님
1111.en: By summer the whole street knows your car — no machine has ever left Steele's gleaming quite like it. He tells every customer you get the finest shine in town. And the others notice one small thing: your key now hangs on its own hook behind his counter, and the shop stays open a little later whenever you drive by.
1111.ko: 여름이 되자 온 거리가 당신 차를 알아봐요. Steele's에서 그렇게 눈부시게 나간 차는 없었거든요. 그는 손님마다 당신이 이 동네에서 제일 좋은 광을 받는다고 말해요. 그리고 다른 사람들은 작은 변화 하나를 눈치채요. 이제 당신 열쇠만 카운터 뒤 전용 고리에 걸려 있고, 당신이 지나가는 날이면 가게가 늘 조금 더 늦게까지 열려 있다는 걸요.

wink.hood: "보닛 열어 봐요, 밑에 뭐가 있나 봐야죠" — 정비의 첫 단계이자, 행간에서는 첫 단추예요. 어느 쪽이든 열긴 열어야 하죠.
wink.underneath: 밑을 봐야 안다는 말은 정비의 기본이에요. 그런데 하필 "수줍어 말고 열어"라는 말과 붙어 나오면, 순수하게만 들리진 않죠.
wink.wax: 왁스는 많이 먹일수록 광이 오래간다는 게 정설이에요. "많이 줄수록 좋아진다"는 말도 — 왁스 얘기라고 Steele 씨는 분명히 말할 거예요.
wink.deep: "깊이 문질러 발라야 오래 간다" — 광택의 정석이에요. 단어를 '깊이'로 고른 것도 사실이고요.
wink.corners: "구석이 제일 중요해요, 진짜 때가 거기 숨어 있거든요." 세차의 진리예요. 하필 구석이라는 게 좀 그렇지만요.
wink.warm: "따뜻할 때 해야 잘 먹어요"는 왁스와 광택엔 실제로 맞는 말이에요. 다만 그 말을 귓가에서 하니 문제죠.
wink.tight: "좁은 곳까지 손이 닿아야 한다"는 디테일링의 자부심이에요. 좁은 곳이라는 표현만 빼면요.
wink.shine: "반짝이게 만들어 드릴게요"는 이 가게의 약속이에요. 무엇을 반짝이게 하는지는 행간에 있죠.

winkEn.hood: "Let's pop the hood and see what's underneath" is step one of any detailing — and, between the lines, step one of a great many other things too. Either way, it has to be opened.
winkEn.underneath: You cannot judge an engine without looking underneath. True enough. It just lands differently right after "don't be shy — open her up."
winkEn.wax: The more wax you work in, the longer the shine lasts — that is genuine car lore. "The more I give her, the better she looks" is also, he will insist, purely about the paint.
winkEn.deep: "Rubbed in deep so it lasts" is textbook polishing advice. It is also true that he chose the word deep.
winkEn.corners: "The corners matter most — that's where the real dirt hides." A sacred truth of car washing. The word corners is doing a little extra work here.
winkEn.warm: "It takes better while she's still warm" is honestly correct for wax and polish. The trouble is only that he says it beside your ear.
winkEn.tight: Reaching the tight little spots is the pride of a good detailer. It would sound perfectly innocent if not for the words tight little spots.
winkEn.shine: "I'll make her shine" is this shop's promise. Exactly what gets left shining is a detail that lives between the lines.

detailing.en: a very careful, complete cleaning of a car

detailed.en: cleaned and polished a car with great care

detailers.en: people whose job is careful car cleaning

ride.en: a car, in a casual way

pull.en: to drive into a place and stop

pulling.en: driving into a place, from pull

rag.en: a small cloth for wiping or cleaning

wipes.en: cleans by rubbing with a cloth

machine.en: a made thing with parts that work; here, a car

circle.en: a round shape; a walk all the way around

circling.en: walking around something in a ring

shine.en: to give off bright light; a bright, polished look

shined.en: made bright and glossy, past of shine

gleams.en: shines in a clean, bright way

glows.en: gives off a soft, steady light

glow.en: to give off a soft, steady light

pop.ko: 탁 열다 (pop the hood: 보닛을 열다)
pop.en: to open something quickly, like a car hood

hood.en: the metal cover over a car engine

oil.en: a slippery liquid that keeps an engine running

fingers.en: the long parts at the end of your hand

low.en: not much left; near the bottom

wax.en: a soft shiny coating that protects and polishes

coat.en: a thin layer of something spread on a surface

rubbed.en: pressed and moved on a surface, past of rub

hold.en: hold back means to keep something in, not give fully

customer.en: a person who buys a service

sponge.en: a soft object that soaks up water for washing

curve.en: a smoothly bending line or surface

gap.en: a narrow open space between two things

dirt.en: unclean stuff like dust and mud

reach.en: to get to and touch something

thorough.en: careful and complete, leaving nothing out

polish.en: the work of making a surface shiny; the stuff used

buffs.en: rubs a surface to make it shine

metal.en: a hard shiny material like steel or iron

admire.en: to look at with pleasure and respect

pleasure.en: a happy feeling; My pleasure means you're welcome

reward.en: a good thing you get for doing something well

key.en: a small metal tool that opens locks or starts a car

wink.en: closing one eye for a quick moment as a friendly sign

purrs.en: runs with a soft, smooth sound

easy.en: in a calm way; sleep easy means rest without worry

saturday.en: the day between Friday and Sunday

eager.en: very keen and excited to do something

grins.en: smiles in a big, playful way

relax.en: to rest and let your body go loose

nods.en: moves the head up and down to agree

prefer.en: to like one thing more than another

problem.en: a difficulty; no problem means it is fine

breath.en: the air you breathe; under one's breath means quietly

language.ko: 말, 언어 (speaking my language: 말이 통하다)
language.en: words people use; speaking my language means we agree

bring.en: to make something appear; bring out means reveal

nice.ko: 아주, 좋게 (nice and deep: 아주 깊이)
nice.en: nice and deep means pleasantly deep

behaves.en: acts well; does what it should

admires.en: looks at with pleasure and respect

particular.en: careful about small details; hard to please

shelf.en: a flat board on a wall for holding things

run.en: run low means to have little left

shop.en: a place where work or selling is done

package.en: a set of services sold together

decided.en: made up your mind, past of decide

announced.en: said in a strong, clear way, past of announce

land.en: to come to a place or day

happen.en: happen to means to do by chance

offer.en: to say you will give or do something

treatment.en: the way someone is looked after

strode.en: walked in with big steps, past of stride

poured.en: let flow freely, past of pour

fail.en: without fail means every time, for sure

rushed.en: done too fast, past of rush

doorway.en: the open space of a door

street.en: a road in a town with buildings

quite.en: to a degree; quite like means as much as

hook.en: a bent piece for hanging things

counter.en: a flat table where a shop serves people

later.en: at a time after the usual

getting.en: coming to be; receiving, from get

breathes.en: says softly, as if breathing out

car.en: a machine you drive on roads

care.ko: 돌봄, 정성 (take care of: 잘 봐 주다)

corner.en: a place where two edges meet

dream.en: what you see in your mind while sleeping

finds.en: brings you to a place; discovers

goodbye.en: a word you say when leaving

hear.en: to take in sound; to be told

hello.en: a word you say when you meet someone

leave.en: to let stay; to go away

mirror.en: glass that shows what is before it

mr.en: a title used before a man's name

noon.en: twelve in the middle of the day

notice.en: to see and become aware of

off.en: away; drove off means drove away

past.en: by the side of and beyond

promise.en: words you will keep; to give your word

rush.en: a hurry; no rush means take your time

set.en: set on means firmly decided to

something.en: a thing you do not name

story.ko: 이야기 (tell the story: 그 얘기를 하다)

summer.en: the hot season of the year

wash.en: a cleaning with water; to clean

wave.en: moving a hand to greet or say bye

where.en: at or in the place that

work.en: a job; to do a job
